asymmetry: A 24-year-old doing everything right is being silently pushed into energy debt by a system designed to confuse and overcharge young renters who don't know the rules.
hook: Your energy company is legally robbing you right now.
core_fact: UK energy suppliers can backbill customers for up to 12 months of underestimated usage — meaning a young renter on a 'manageable' direct debit can suddenly owe hundreds of pounds they never budgeted for, with zero warning and full legal backing.
villain: Ofgem and the Big Six energy suppliers — who set artificially low direct debits to win customers, then claw back the difference in lump-sum debt demands.
share_trigger: Did you see this? This is exactly what happened to me last winter — they owe us an explanation.
search_query: UK energy bill debt direct debit underestimate Ofgem overcharge young renters 2024
